reasoning: Butterfly is a Type (Substantial Sortal). It is rigid (+R) because an entity that is a butterfly remains one for its entire existence; it cannot cease being a butterfly without ceasing to exist. It supplies its own identity conditions (+O, +I) allowing us to distinguish one butterfly from another. It possesses unity (+U) as it is a discrete living organism (a whole), and it is not inherently dependent (-D) on the existence of another specific individual entity in the way a role like 'Student' or 'Husband' is.

Red is non-rigid because a red entity can change color without losing its identity. It does not carry or supply unique identity conditions (we do not identify objects based on 'redness' alone), and it is non-unifying as it can apply to both wholes and parts of wholes.

Food is a role played by entities (like fruit or meat) rather than an essential property; an object can cease to be food without losing its identity. It does not provide its own identity conditions (we identify 'this apple' as food, not 'this food' as an apple) and it is externally dependent on a consumer for which it serves as nourishment.

Animal is a rigid property because an instance cannot cease to be an animal without ceasing to exist. It supplies its own identity conditions (IC) for its instances and characterizes them as intrinsic wholes (unity). It is considered independent as it does not require the existence of another specific entity to exist, unlike a role.

RedApple is non-rigid because a specific apple can cease to be red (e.g., rot or be painted) without losing its identity as an apple. It carries identity (+I) inherited from the type 'Apple', but does not supply its own unique identity condition (-O). It is a whole (+U) and exists independently (-D).

A Group is a Rigid property because a group cannot cease to be a group without losing its identity. It supplies its own identity condition (+O) based on its membership and structure. It is Anti-Unity (~U) because it is strictly an aggregate or collective of distinct parts rather than a single topological whole. It is Dependent (+D) because a group's existence necessitates the existence of its members.

A Country is a rigid property because an instance of a country cannot cease to be a country without losing its identity. It supplies its own identity conditions (defined by borders, sovereignty, and international law) and implies unity as it is considered an intrinsic whole (a sovereign entity).

AmountOfMatter is Rigid because an amount of matter cannot cease to be an amount of matter without ceasing to exist. It is -I/-O because it lacks a clear Identity Condition for individuals (it is cumulative; the sum of two amounts of matter is still just an amount of matter). It is Anti-Unity (~U) because amounts of matter are divisible and do not possess an intrinsic whole-part structure (unlike discrete objects).

PhysicalObject is a rigid property because an entity that is a physical object cannot cease to be one without ceasing to exist. It supplies its own globally unique identity conditions (+O, +I) based on spatial occupation and material continuity. It carries a unity condition (+U) because physical objects are perceived as discrete, integral wholes, and it is inherently independent (-D).

A GroupOfPeople is rigid because its members cannot cease to be a group and remain the same entity. It supplies its own identity (e.g., via membership criteria), but it is anti-unified (~U) because its instances are mereological sums/aggregates of individuals rather than intrinsic wholes. It is dependent (+D) because the group cannot exist without the people that compose it.

LivingBeing is a Rigid property because an entity that is alive cannot cease to be a living being without ceasing to exist as that entity. it provides a primary identity condition (+O) forits instances, distinguishing them from inanimate matter, and implies Unity (+U) because a living being is a functional, integrated whole.

SocialEntity is likely rigid (+R) because if an entity is inherently social in nature, it cannot cease to be so without losing its identity. It carries identity (+I) inherited from its children (like Organizations or Societies) but is too general to provide a single global identity condition (+O). It is non-unifying (-U) because social entities can be individuals, groups, or abstract structures with different internal bounds. It is dependent (+D) because social entities cannot exist without an underlying population of agents.

Location is treated as a rigid property (if something is a location, it is essentially a location), but it is generally considered a non-sortal because it lacks its own specific criteria for distinguishing individuals without spatial-temporal coordinates. It does not provide a global identity condition for the entities occupying it, nor does it guarantee unity, as locations can be fragmented or continuous regions.

Fruit is a Rigid property because a specific specimen cannot cease to be a fruit without losing its identity (it is an essential biological category). It is not a Type (+O) because there is no unique identity condition common to all fruits that isn't already supplied by more specific types like Apple or Orange (it is a dispersive category). It carries Unity (+U) because a fruit is recognized as a discrete whole object.

Vertebrate is a rigid property because an organism cannot cease to be a vertebrate without ceasing to exist. It carries identity (+I) inherited from the higher-level concept 'Animal', but typically does not supply a unique global identity condition (+O) distinct from the animal kingdom. Instances are intrinsic wholes (+U) and are not existentially dependent on another specific entity to exist (-D).

Caterpillar is a Phase property. It is anti-rigid (~R) because an entity can cease being a caterpillar (by becoming a butterfly) without losing its identity as a living organism. It carries identity (+I) but does not supply its own (+O), as it inherits identity conditions from the rigid type 'Organism' (or 'Lepidoptera'). It possesses unity (+U) as instances are functional wholes, and is independent (-D) as it does not require an external entity to exist, unlike a 'Student' or 'Employee'.

Being a person is essential to all its instances (rigidity). It provides a unique global identity condition for its instances (own identity) and defines what constitutes a whole instance (unity). It does not inherently depend on another entity to exist (independence).

LegalAgent is anti-rigid (~R) because an entity (like a Person or Corporation) can cease to be a legal agent without losing its identity. It is a Role because it carries identity (+I) inherited from an underlying Sortal but does not provide its own unique identity condition (-O). It is dependent (+D) as it requires a legal system and an underlying entity to exist.

As the most general category, 'Entity' is rigid because if something is an entity, it is so in every possible world. However, it is a Non-Sortal because it does not carry or supply a specific identity condition (common to all its members) nor does it provide a unity criterion, as an entity can be either a discrete whole or an aggregate.

Organization is a Rigid property (+R) because an instance of an organization cannot stop being an organization without ceasing to exist. It supplies its own identity condition (+O, +I), typically defined by legal registration or social charter, ensuring it can be distinguished from other entities. It possesses unity (+U) as it is perceived as a whole with specific boundaries and internal structure. It is externally dependent (+D) because it requires the existence of constituent members or legal frameworks to exist.

The term 'Agent' is anti-rigid (~R) because it is a role an entity plays; no entity is an agent in all possible worlds. It carries identity (+I) inherited from its base type (like Person or Robot) but does not supply its own (+O). It is externally dependent (+D) because being an agent necessitates a context, action, or principal.

As a substance sortal, an apple is rigid (+R) because if it ceases to be an apple, it ceases to exist as that entity. It provides its own identity condition (+O) for distinguishing one apple from another and carries unity (+U) because an apple is an intrinsic whole with a well-defined boundary.
